overview.tagline: A vibrant borough with a beloved walkable downtown, train service, and small-town charm
overview.description: The Borough of Westwood is one of Bergen County's most charming communities, anchored by a thriving walkable downtown along Broadway and Westwood Avenue. With NJ Transit train service, excellent schools through the Westwood Regional district, and an active calendar of community events, Westwood attracts families and young professionals who value a true town-center lifestyle. The borough successfully balances suburban residential neighborhoods with a lively commercial core.
highlights: Vibrant walkable downtown with independent restaurants, shops, and cafes
NJ Transit Main/Bergen County Line train station
Highly rated Westwood Regional School District
Active community calendar with street fairs, concerts, and seasonal events
Classic small-town atmosphere with tree-lined residential streets

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Westwood's housing stock includes a charming mix of older Colonials, Tudors, Cape Cods, and newer townhomes.
realEstate.recentTrends: Westwood has seen strong demand driven by its walkable downtown, train service, and school quality. Homes near the train station and downtown command premium prices. The market is competitive with limited inventory, and prices have appreciated steadily in recent years.
neighborhoodNotes: Downtown/Broadway area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and the train station
Kinderkamack Road section — Established homes along the main north-south route
Third Avenue area — Family-friendly neighborhood near parks and schools
Irvington Street section — Tree-lined streets with well-maintained homes
West side — Quiet residential area near the Washington Township border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Recycling bins available from the borough
Electronics and hazardous waste events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water / Suez
Sewer service maintained by Borough DPW
Quarterly billing for water and sewer
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Downtown historic character considered in commercial approvals

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates commuter parking and manages downtown parking for the business district.
details: Commuter parking permits available for the train station lot
Metered and time-limited parking in the downtown business district
Municipal parking lots on Broadway and Westwood Avenue
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
ParkMobile app accepted in some areas

additionalServices.title: Westwood Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering extensive collections, programs, and community spaces.
details: Located at 49 Park Avenue
Children's story time, teen programs, and adult book clubs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and study rooms

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer camps
Adult fitness classes and senior programs
Community events and seasonal celebrations
Facilities at Veterans Memorial Park and other locations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Westwood enforces noise regulations to maintain quality of life in residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers restricted to certain hours and seasons
Amplified music restricted after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with height limits — 6 feet rear, 4 feet front
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Property exteriors must be maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Westwood requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Maximum number of pets per household may apply
No exotic or dangerous animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated throughout the borough, particularly in the downtown and near the train station.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Time limits in the downtown business district
Commuter lot requires a permit
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles prohibited from residential streets overnight

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability may be required at change of tenancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Fire safety inspections for multi-family dwellings
Short-term rentals subject to borough regulations

other.title: Downtown Business District Regulations
other.summary: Special regulations apply to businesses in the downtown district to maintain its character.
keyRules: Signage must comply with downtown design standards
Outdoor dining permitted with proper approval
Building facades must be maintained to commercial standards
Special events require borough approval and permits

publicSchools.name: Westwood Regional High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school shared with Washington Township; strong academics and diverse extracurriculars

publicSchools.name: Westwood Regional Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Regional middle school serving Westwood and Washington Township students

publicSchools.name: Berkeley Avenue School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving Westwood students in grades K-3

publicSchools.name: Jessie F. George School
publicSchools.notes: Intermediate school serving Westwood students in grades 4-6

privateSchools.name: St. Andrew's Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school serving the Westwood and Washington Township area

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Vibrant walkable downtown with restaurants, shops, and cafes
NJ Transit train station for convenient NYC commuting
Highly rated Westwood Regional School District
Strong sense of community with year-round events
Classic small-town character with tree-lined streets
Moderate home prices with excellent amenities

commuteToNYC.duration: 50-65 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Westwood Station (on Broadway near the downtown)
commuteToNYC.notes: Westwood has its own NJ Transit train station on the Main/Bergen County Line, providing direct service to Hoboken Terminal where commuters connect via PATH or ferry to Manhattan. The station is conveniently located in the downtown. Bus service is also available via Routes 165 and 175. Total commute to Midtown Manhattan is approximately 50-65 minutes.

costOfLiving.summary: Westwood offers a moderate cost of living for Bergen County, with housing prices that provide excellent value given the walkable downtown, train service, and school quality.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 25% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Walkable downtown with thriving restaurant and shop scene
Own NJ Transit train station for NYC commuting
Strong school district with K-12 in the community
Active community events throughout the year
cons: Train commute requires transfer at Hoboken for Manhattan
Downtown parking can be limited during peak times
Property taxes are significant by national standards
Compact borough with smaller lot sizes
Some commercial traffic through the downtown area

restaurants.name: Tina Louise
restaurants.description: Popular modern American restaurant and bar offering creative cocktails, seasonal dishes, and a lively atmosphere in the heart of downtown.

restaurants.name: Bibim
restaurants.description: Trendy Korean restaurant serving authentic Korean cuisine including bibimbap, Korean fried chicken, and small plates.

restaurants.name: Portobello
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering classic pasta dishes, wood-fired pizza, and Italian specialties in a warm setting.

restaurants.name: Baumgart's Cafe
restaurants.description: Unique restaurant known for its creative fusion of Chinese-American and Continental cuisine, a Westwood institution.

restaurants.name: Jade Eatery & Lounge
restaurants.description: Modern Asian-fusion restaurant offering sushi, dim sum, and creative cocktails in a stylish setting.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Westwood's primary park featuring sports fields, a playground, walking track, basketball courts, and a memorial honoring local veterans.

parks.name: Pascack Brook Park
parks.description: Scenic park along Pascack Brook with walking trails, benches, and natural areas for relaxation.

parks.name: Harrington Park (nearby)
parks.description: Bergen County park with sports fields and open green space accessible from Westwood.

events.name: Westwood Street Fair
events.description: Major annual street fair along Broadway featuring vendors, food, live music, rides, and activities for the whole family.

events.name: Westwood Summer Concert Series
events.description: Free outdoor concerts in Veterans Memorial Park throughout the summer months.

events.name: Halloween Parade & Fall Festival
events.description: Annual Halloween parade through downtown followed by a fall festival with activities and prizes.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Community celebration with tree lighting, caroling, festive shopping, and holiday spirit in the downtown.

attractions.name: Downtown Westwood
attractions.description: Walkable downtown district along Broadway and Westwood Avenue featuring over 100 independent restaurants, boutiques, cafes, and service businesses.

attractions.name: Westwood Cinema
attractions.description: Local movie theater in the downtown area providing first-run films in an intimate, community setting.

attractions.name: Westwood Free Public Library
attractions.description: Community library offering a welcoming space for reading, programs, and cultural events.

shoppingAreas: Broadway — Main downtown shopping and dining street
Westwood Avenue — Additional shops and restaurants
Center Avenue — Local businesses and services
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major mall 10 minutes south

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 1st and 3rd Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 101 Washington Avenue, Westwood, NJ 07675

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
